violations.text: Working containers of liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Staff labeled during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
The bar dish machine failed to pull sanitizer at the proper concentration. Service was called and replaced line during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2025

Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink at the dish wash area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2025

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2025

Raw animal foods (beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC identified and relocated items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2025

Prep unit was observed holding an ambient of 53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff moved all foods requiring refrigeration to other units; service was called. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2025

Working containers of liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Containers of potatoes and squash were observed stored on the floor in the dry storage room. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. PIC instructed staff to obtain hair restraints. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2025
The dish machine at the bar failed to dispense sanitizer. PIC informed; service called. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2025
